skill.name: context-compression
skill.description: Design structured context compression workflows for long AI agent sessions, with emphasis on preserving file history, decisions, and next steps.
skill.summary: Compress long agent sessions while preserving task-critical context.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a high score because markdown fences, examples, and words such as system were misclassified as shell execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, or sensitive data. Manual review found no prompt injection, no executable shell backticks, no credential access, and no malicious network or filesystem behavior. The included Python script is a local evaluation helper with a commented API example and heuristic scoring.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer identified markdown fences and an illustrative Python API snippet as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Manual review found documentation templates and a commented production note, not live shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced markdown examples or a commented code sample. No executable shell invocation or user-controlled command construction was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Compression Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography alerts appear to match context-compression terminology and example prose. The reviewed files do not implement hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic verification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations discuss compression quality, evaluation dimensions, and example summaries. There is no cryptographic API usage or weak algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System and Sensitive Data Terms Used as Examples
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance and Windows SAM alerts are triggered by ordinary evaluation examples, configuration-file references, and a role name in a commented API call. No code reads host identity, scans the system, or accesses sensitive operating-system files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited terms are part of examples and scoring logic. I found no file access to SAM databases, no system enumeration commands, and no exfiltration path.

content.value_statement: Long agent sessions lose important details when summaries are too aggressive. This skill provides structured compression methods that preserve files, decisions, state, and next steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains anchored iterative summarization for long-running agent sessions.
Defines summary sections for intent, files, decisions, current state, and next steps.
Compares anchored, opaque, and regenerative compression strategies.
Provides probe types for testing recall, artifacts, continuation, and decisions.
Includes a Python helper for local compression-quality evaluation.
Frames success around tokens per completed task, not only tokens per request.
limitations: Does not provide a production-ready tokenizer or hosted evaluation service.
The Python evaluator uses heuristic scoring unless a production judge is added.
Opaque compression is discussed conceptually, not implemented as a codec.
Reported benchmark values are reference material and should be validated locally.

use_cases.title: Preserve Coding Session State
use_cases.description: Create structured summaries that keep modified files, failing tests, and pending fixes visible after context compaction.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers using AI coding assistants

use_cases.title: Design Agent Memory Workflows
use_cases.description: Choose compression triggers and summary sections for long agent runs that must continue across many turns.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Summary Quality
use_cases.description: Use recall, artifact, continuation, and decision probes to compare compression strategies before adoption.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Session
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a structured summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress this conversation into a structured summary with intent, files changed, decisions, current state, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Compression Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for an agent workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare anchored iterative, opaque, and regenerative compression for this workflow. Recommend one strategy and explain the trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Build Evaluation Probes
prompt_templates.prompt: Create recall, artifact, continuation, and decision probes for this compressed context. Include expected answers and scoring guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Context Loss
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert review after a failed continuation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this failed continuation and identify which details were lost during compression. Propose revised summary sections and trigger rules.

output_examples.input: A long debugging conversation reached the context limit after multiple file reads, fixes, and test runs.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a structured summary with the original issue, files modified, files read, root cause, test status, decisions, and remaining actions.

output_examples.input: A platform team wants to compare three compression methods before changing its agent runtime.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends evaluation probes, explains quality dimensions, and compares methods using task continuity and artifact tracking.

output_examples.input: An agent forgot which files it changed after compaction.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies artifact trail loss and recommends a dedicated file-state section or separate artifact index.

best_practices: Optimize for total tokens per completed task, not only summary size.
Use explicit sections for files, decisions, current state, and next steps.
Test compression quality with probes before relying on it in production.
anti_patterns: Do not compress away file paths, function names, errors, or test results.
Do not regenerate the full summary every time if incremental merging is needed.
Do not rely on lexical similarity metrics alone for functional quality.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps compress long AI agent sessions without losing the context needed to continue work.

faq.question: Is this only for coding agents?
faq.answer: No. It is most detailed for coding agents, but the structured summary approach can support other long workflows.

faq.question: Does it implement automatic context compression?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and a local evaluation helper. Production integration requires agent-runtime work.

faq.question: Why not always maximize compression ratio?
faq.answer: Maximum compression can lose details that force re-reading and rework, which increases total task cost.

faq.question: How should teams evaluate results?
faq.answer: They should ask targeted probes about facts, files, next steps, and decisions after compression.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The included evaluator runs locally by default, and the API judge call is only a commented production note.
